```text
1	# attend
2	
3	Local web dashboard for brief-based AI session management across Claude Code + Codex.
4	
5	> session = cache, brief = state. vendor = replaceable backend, vault = substrate.
6	
7	## What it does
8	
9	- Scans your vaults for `brief.md` files (one per task)
10	- Reads Claude Code JSONL transcripts and matches them to briefs via `cwd`
11	- Derives per-brief telemetry: sessions / prompts / actions / dwell / last touch
12	- Classifies each brief into a behavioral pattern (avoidance / stalled / active / healthy / fresh)
13	- Ranks briefs by `memory-alignment × pattern × explicit blocker`
14	- Serves one localhost page you can poll instead of N IDE tabs
15	
16	## Quick start
17	
18	```powershell
19	cd D:\workspace\projects\attend
20	pip install -r requirements.txt
21	python daemon.py
22	```
23	
24	Open http://localhost:5050.
25	
26	## Brief format
27	
28	Each task gets a `brief.md` at `projects/<name>/brief.md`:
29	
30	```markdown
31	---
32	status: active            # active | deferred | done
33	last_touch: 2026-05-30
34	defer_until:              # condition, not date — e.g. "等 X 回复"
35	---
36	
37	## what
38	一句话, 加 why (为什么这事值得做).
39	
40	## accept
41	done 的判据 (不是 todo list).
42	
43	## next
44	现在卡在哪 / 下一步该试什么.
45	每次离开 session 前更新这一行.
46	```
47	
48	Only `what` / `accept` / `next` sections are read. Other sections are fine — they're ignored.
49	
50	## Config
51	
52	Edit `CONFIG` at the top of `daemon.py`:
53	
54	- `vault_roots` — paths scanned recursively for `brief.md`
55	- `claude_projects` — `~/.claude/projects` (default fits Claude Code)
56	- `codex_sessions` — `~/.codex/sessions` (optional, currently not parsed in v0)
57	- `memory_file` — your Claude Code MEMORY.md for keyword extraction
58	- `port` — default 5050
59	
60	## Pattern definitions
61	
62	| Pattern | Trigger | Meaning |
63	|---|---|---|
64	| `fresh` | 0 sessions | Brief exists, no work started |
65	| `avoidance` | ≥5 prompts, 0 actions | Many entries without productive output — possible decision avoidance |
66	| `stalled` | 0 actions, last touch ≥ 7 days | Cold, no recent activity |
67	| `healthy` | actions > 0, avg dwell ≥ 10 min, touch ≤ 3 days | In flow, don't interrupt |
68	| `active` | some actions, otherwise unclassified | Generic in-progress |
69	
70	Telemetry is **descriptive, never judgmental** (Steel 2007 on procrastination self-report → avoidance feedback loops). All pattern labels are observations, not verdicts.
71	
72	## Priority scoring (v0 heuristic)
73	
74	`score = (memory alignment × 2) + pattern weight + explicit blocker bonus − defer/done penalty`
75	
76	Each brief gets a one-line reason explaining the rank. Reasons compose so you can override.
77	
78	## Spawn commands
79	
80	Detail page generates copy-paste commands for both Claude and Codex.
81	**v0 does not auto-spawn** — it copies to clipboard, you paste to your terminal.
82	This is intentional: auto-spawn from dashboard → 4-tab cascade.
83	
84	## What this is NOT (deliberately)
85	
86	- Not a chat UI — sessions stay in Claude Code / Codex
87	- Not push notifications — pull, manual refresh
88	- Not a TODO app — briefs are task-shaped, not item-shaped
89	- Not multi-user — single-person attention router
90	- Not Obsidian / Zettelkasten — flat folder scan, no bidirectional links
91	
92	## v0 → v1 roadmap
93	
94	- [ ] Codex JSONL telemetry (when schema known)
95	- [ ] Split CLI: `attend split <jsonl-path>` → N candidate briefs
96	- [ ] Optional LLM-based priority (env-flag, with reasons)
97	- [ ] `attend new <name>` to scaffold brief.md
98	- [ ] Activity heatmap per brief (dwell over time)
99	- [ ] Brief edit in-browser (currently file-system only)
100	
101	## Design invariants
102	
103	Touch these only with cause:
104	
105	1. **brief = state, session = cache** — nothing the dashboard knows lives only in a session
106	2. **pull, not push** — no notifications unless user opts in later
107	3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** — Steel constraint
108	4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** — JSONL is read; spawn is per-vendor
109	5. **single polling surface** — adding a second dashboard is a regression
110
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# attend — design
2	
3	This document captures the design context that led to v0. Subsequent sessions opened in this project should read this first to onboard.
4	
5	## Problem
6	
7	A single person collaborating with AI faces four compounding costs:
8	
9	1. **Concurrency** — 10+ active sessions across Claude Code (multiple VS Code projects) + Codex CLI is normal, not pathological. Reducing the count is not the goal.
10	2. **Vendor isolation** — Claude Code and Codex are separate ecosystems. `/fork` exists in each (Codex CLI has it, Claude Code CLI has it but the VS Code panel does not). Neither is cross-vendor. Manual copy-paste is the current state.
11	3. **Decision avoidance** — when a session needs a hard decision, the user switches to easier completed sessions to "verify and push." Classic Steel-type *avoidant procrastination*. Self-report doesn't detect it (gets rationalized as "efficient batching"). Behavioral telemetry would.
12	4. **Polling overhead** — N IDE tabs × re-orientation cost on each switch (UC Irvine: 23 min 15 s to refocus; APA: 40% productivity loss from switching). Adding more organization tools makes this worse, not better.
13	
14	## Conceptual model — the invariant
15	
16	```
17	session = cache       (transient, vendor-bound, may die)
18	brief   = state       (persistent, vendor-neutral, survives session death)
19	vendor  = replaceable backend
20	vault   = substrate
21	```
22	
23	A task may use 0..N sessions over its life. Sessions are vehicles; briefs are passengers. **A cold 3-day-old session is usually more expensive to resume than spawning a fresh session from a brief** — chat history accumulates dead context, summarization loses fidelity, and the user's own working memory of the session has decayed.
24	
25	Concrete consequence: don't try to keep sessions alive across attention gaps. Externalize state to brief; kill the session; spawn fresh when ready.
26	
27	## Four components
28	
29	### 1. Brief
30	
31	- **Granularity**: per-task, not per-session
32	- **Location**: `projects/<name>/brief.md` — physically colocated with work, not in a separate "briefs/" directory. Found by `ls`, not by remembering.
33	- **Three sections**:
34	  - `what` — one sentence + *why* this is worth doing
35	  - `accept` — done criteria (not a TODO list)
36	  - `next` — **where you are stuck / what to try next**. Update this line before every session-leave. Gollwitzer's implementation-intention research: the meta-decision ("what should I do?") is where cost concentrates; pre-committing it at cheap time pays off massively.
37	- **Front matter**: `status` (active/deferred/done), `defer_until` (a *condition*, not a date — "等 X 回复"), `last_touch`
38	- **Lifecycle**: created manually or via split; killed only by explicit `status: done`. Cold sessions are killed; their briefs persist.
39	
40	### 2. Telemetry
41	
42	- **Data sources**: vendor JSONL transcripts
43	  - Claude Code: `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl`
44	  - Codex CLI: `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl` (not parsed in v0)
45	- **Session-to-brief matching**: by `cwd` field in JSONL. A session's cwd contains (or equals) the brief's project directory.
46	- **Per-brief signals**: sessions count, prompts count, action count (Edit/Write/Bash tool uses), dwell distribution, last_touch_age, last_action_age
47	- **Pattern classifier**:
48	
49	| Pattern | Trigger | What it means |
50	|---|---|---|
51	| `fresh` | 0 sessions | Brief exists, no work started |
52	| `avoidance` | ≥5 prompts, 0 actions | Many entries, no productive output — decision is what's needed, not more "work" |
53	| `stalled` | 0 actions, last touch ≥ 7 days | Cold — needs unblock or kill |
54	| `healthy` | actions > 0, avg dwell ≥ 10 min, touch ≤ 3 days | In flow, don't interrupt |
55	| `active` | actions > 0, otherwise | Generic in-progress |
56	
57	- **Hard constraint** — descriptive, never judgmental (Steel 2007: judgmental feedback on procrastination behavior *worsens* it via self-esteem mediation). Output strings must avoid second-person pressure verbs ("you again," "still stuck"). Use observation-form: "X count, Y duration, Z actions."
58	
59	### 3. Priority evaluator
60	
61	- **Inputs**: brief × user-memory keywords (extracted from MEMORY.md) × telemetry pattern × explicit blockers in `next`
62	- **Output**: ranked list + **one-line reason per rank** (no opaque scores; user must be able to override)
63	- **Time NOT used as input** — explicitly rejected (see below). Semantic alignment with memory is the real axis; pattern signals action implication.
64	- v0: heuristic scoring (memory keyword hits × 2 + pattern bonus + blocker bonus − defer penalty)
65	- v1+: LLM-based, env-flagged (`ATTEND_LLM=anthropic|openai`)
66	
67	### 4. Split (not in v0)
68	
69	- **Purpose**: when a session organically grows N follow-up threads, factor them out into N new briefs
70	- **Flow**: read transcript → LLM "identify open follow-up threads" → N candidate briefs in 3-line format → user reviews → commits selected to vault
71	- **Intentional limitation**: does NOT auto-spawn sessions. spawn is a separate user decision. Auto-spawn from split would create a 4-tab cascade — exactly the load problem we're solving.
72	- **Distinction from fork**: fork = duplicate-and-diverge (Warp, Claude SDK, Codex CLI); split = factor-out. Fork copies state; split extracts task specs. They're different operations.
73	- **Cross-vendor**: split reads JSONL on disk — vendor-neutral input → vendor-neutral brief output. Vendor only re-enters at spawn time.
74	
75	## Product form
76	
77	**Local web app at `http://localhost:5050`. Python + Flask + server-side HTML.** Daemon scans vault, reads vendor JSONL, renders ranked feed. Polled by browser refresh.
78	
79	### Why this form
80	
81	- **Single polling surface** — one tab, one URL. Replaces N IDE tabs.
82	- **Vendor-neutral** — not bound to VS Code or any terminal. Treats Claude and Codex equally.
83	- **Data in local vault** — daemon is view-only; if it dies the vault is still authoritative.
84	- **Incremental enrichment** — start static HTML, add buttons (spawn, defer, split) as needed.
85	
86	### Forms rejected
87	
88	- **VS Code plugin** — locks one IDE; can't span Claude + Codex
89	- **TUI** — multi-window management collapses back to the tab-bounce problem
90	- **Desktop app** — engineering overhead without benefit
91	- **Push notifications** — user confirmed: when polling = idle moments, push doesn't help (can't act when not idle). Pull-only.
92	
93	### Inspirational reference
94	
95	Slock.ai's structural primitives — channel/thread (persistent, task-shaped) vs. message stream (transient) — directly informed brief/session separation. We use the structural insight without copying the multi-agent collaboration features.
96	
97	## Design invariants
98	
99	Touch these only with explicit cause:
100	
101	1. **brief = state, session = cache** — nothing the dashboard knows lives only in a session
102	2. **pull, not push** — no notifications unless user opts in
103	3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** — Steel constraint, see telemetry section
104	4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** — JSONL is read; spawn is per-vendor
105	5. **single polling surface** — adding a second dashboard is a regression
106	
107	## Rejected paths (don't redo)
108	
109	- **4-bucket organization** (TODO / 进行中 / 已完成 / 长期保留 in separate locations). Asks user to remember N locations. Replaced by brief sidecar at task location, found by `ls`.
110	- **Obsidian bidirectional links / Zettelkasten-style PKM**. Explicitly deferred — user judgment was that base models + memory + agent platforms will deliver this upstream over time. Don't preempt; revisit if upstream stalls.
111	- **Hot/warm/cold by time**. Session state changes fast but total count stays high; recency doesn't track priority. Replaced by memory-aligned semantic priority + pattern-based action implication.
112	- **LangChain Agent Inbox / push notifications**. User's diagnostic cost when polling is already near-zero; push adds noise without enabling action.
113	- **Cap to 2-5 concurrent sessions**. Open ≠ active. Goal isn't reducing tab count, it's reducing re-warming cost via durable briefs.
114	- **Fork as cross-vendor primitive**. Each vendor's fork is locked to its transcript format and API. Brief is the cross-vendor abstraction; fork lives one level below.
115	- **Auto-spawn from dashboard**. Auto-spawning new sessions from priority list would recreate the cascade we just dismantled. Spawn buttons copy commands only.
116	
117	## Cross-vendor data layer
118	
119	Both vendors store transcripts as local JSONL with a `cwd` field. Daemon reads both, matches sessions to briefs by cwd-vs-project-dir containment, derives signals per brief, renders vendor-neutral output. Vendor only re-appears at:
120	
121	1. **Transcript ingestion** (per-vendor JSONL parser)
122	2. **Spawn command** (per-vendor CLI invocation)
123	
124	Everything in between (briefs, dashboard, priority, telemetry) is vendor-agnostic markdown/HTML.
125	
126	## Research touchstones
127	
128	- **Steel, P. (2007)** — *Arousal, avoidant, and decisional procrastinators: do they exist?* Three procrastination types; foundational for the avoidance pattern definition. Identified behavioral-measurement gap: most procrastination research is self-report, telemetry is largely unexplored. This is the niche the telemetry component fills.
129	- **Gollwitzer, P. (1999)** — *Implementation intentions.* Pre-committing "if X then Y" at cheap time reduces decision-point friction. Direct rationale for the `next` line discipline.
130	- **Rosenbaum (2014)** — *Pre-crastination.* Doing the easy thing first to free working memory is a real mechanism, not always avoidance. Distinguishes by whether the easy task ends in 1-2 hours; if longer, it's likely the avoidance pattern.
131	- **Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern** — `wiki/hot.md` of ~500 words auto-loaded at session start. Brief is our hot-cache analog.
132	- **Slock.ai** — Persistent channels with transient message streams. Structural inspiration for brief/session split.
133	
134	## v0 → v1 roadmap
135	
136	- [ ] Codex JSONL parser (need a sample of Codex's schema; user has it via CLI but not yet active)
137	- [ ] `attend split <jsonl-path>` CLI: LLM extracts open follow-up threads → N candidate briefs to review
138	- [ ] `attend new <project>` to scaffold `projects/<project>/brief.md`
139	- [ ] LLM-based priority option (env-flag)
140	- [ ] Refined blocker regex (only `等[具体内容]`, not bare `等` char — currently triggers false positives on parentheticals like "(作品B 等)")
141	- [ ] Dwell distribution heatmap per brief
142	- [ ] In-browser brief edit (currently file-system only)
143	
144	## Out of scope (likely permanently)
145	
146	- Multi-user collaboration (Slock-style server/channel/DM)
147	- Real-time push / SSE / websocket
148	- Chat UI in the dashboard (sessions live in Claude Code / Codex)
149	- Hierarchical task trees (briefs are flat; relations live in the `next` line as references)
150	- Cloud sync (vault is local; OneDrive sync of the vault is incidental, not a feature)
151	
152	## How a future session onboards
153	
154	If you (Claude / Codex / human) are picking this up cold:
155	
156	1. Read this document end-to-end.
157	2. Read `README.md` for run instructions.
158	3. Read `brief.md` for current `next`.
159	4. Open http://localhost:5050 if the daemon is running (`python daemon.py`).
160	5. Pick from the brief's `next` and start.
161	
162	Do not add organizational layers unless you've checked them against invariant 5. Do not change telemetry output to be more "motivating" — that's invariant 3 violation; see Steel research.
163
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""attend — local web dashboard for brief-based AI session management.
2	
3	session = cache, brief = state.
4	vendor (Claude / Codex) = replaceable backend, vault = substrate.
5	"""
6	
7	import json
8	import re
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	import yaml
13	from flask import Flask, render_template, request
14	
15	# ----- config -----
16	
17	HOME = Path.home()
18	
19	CONFIG = {
20	    "vault_roots": [
21	        HOME / "OneDrive" / "notes",
22	        Path("D:/workspace/projects"),
23	    ],
24	    "claude_projects": HOME / ".claude" / "projects",
25	    "codex_sessions": HOME / ".codex" / "sessions",
26	    "memory_file": HOME / ".claude" / "projects" / "c--Users-sinph-OneDrive-notes" / "memory" / "MEMORY.md",
27	    "port": 5050,
28	    "scan_depth": 6,
29	}
30	
31	# ----- brief scanning -----
32	
33	def parse_brief(path):
34	    try:
35	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
36	    except OSError:
37	        return None
38	    fm = {}
39	    body = text
40	    if text.startswith("---"):
41	        end = text.find("\n---", 3)
42	        if end > 0:
43	            try:
44	                fm = yaml.safe_load(text[3:end]) or {}
45	            except yaml.YAMLError:
46	                fm = {}
47	            body = text[end + 4:].lstrip()
48	    sections = {}
49	    current = None
50	    buf = []
51	    for line in body.splitlines():
52	        m = re.match(r"^#+\s+(\S+)", line)
53	        if m:
54	            if current is not None:
55	                sections[current] = "\n".join(buf).strip()
56	            current = m.group(1).lower()
57	            buf = []
58	        else:
59	            buf.append(line)
60	    if current is not None:
61	        sections[current] = "\n".join(buf).strip()
62	    project_dir = path.parent
63	    return {
64	        "path": str(path),
65	        "project_dir": str(project_dir),
66	        "name": project_dir.name,
67	        "front_matter": fm,
68	        "what": sections.get("what", ""),
69	        "accept": sections.get("accept", ""),
70	        "next": sections.get("next", ""),
71	        "status": (fm.get("status") or "active"),
72	        "defer_until": fm.get("defer_until"),
73	    }
74	
75	def scan_vault():
76	    briefs = []
77	    seen = set()
78	    for root in CONFIG["vault_roots"]:
79	        if not root.exists():
80	            continue
81	        for path in root.rglob("brief.md"):
82	            sp = str(path).lower()
83	            if sp in seen:
84	                continue
85	            seen.add(sp)
86	            b = parse_brief(path)
87	            if b:
88	                briefs.append(b)
89	    return briefs
90	
91	# ----- claude telemetry -----
92	
93	def _parse_ts(s):
94	    if not s:
95	        return None
96	    try:
97	        return datetime.fromisoformat(s.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
98	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
99	        return None
100	
101	def _scan_claude_sessions():
102	    """Yield (jsonl_path, cwd, first_ts, last_ts, prompt_count, action_count)."""
103	    root = CONFIG["claude_projects"]
104	    if not root.exists():
105	        return
106	    for proj_dir in root.iterdir():
107	        if not proj_dir.is_dir():
108	            continue
109	        for jsonl in proj_dir.glob("*.jsonl"):
110	            cwd = None
111	            first_ts = None
112	            last_ts = None
113	            prompts = 0
114	            actions = 0
115	            try:
116	                with jsonl.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
117	                    for line in f:
118	                        try:
119	                            obj = json.loads(line)
120	                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
121	                            continue
122	                        if cwd is None and obj.get("cwd"):
123	                            cwd = obj["cwd"]
124	                        ts = _parse_ts(obj.get("timestamp"))
125	                        if ts:
126	                            if first_ts is None:
127	                                first_ts = ts
128	                            last_ts = ts
129	                        t = obj.get("type")
130	                        if t == "user":
131	                            msg = obj.get("message") or {}
132	                            content = msg.get("content") if isinstance(msg, dict) else None
133	                            if isinstance(content, str) and content.strip() and not content.startswith("<"):
134	                                prompts += 1
135	                            elif isinstance(content, list):
136	                                for c in content:
137	                                    if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "text":
138	                                        prompts += 1
139	                                        break
140	                        elif t == "assistant":
141	                            msg = obj.get("message") or {}
142	                            if isinstance(msg, dict):
143	                                for c in msg.get("content", []) or []:
144	                                    if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "tool_use":
145	                                        name = c.get("name", "")
146	                                        if name in ("Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit", "Bash", "PowerShell"):
147	                                            actions += 1
148	            except OSError:
149	                continue
150	            yield {
151	                "path": str(jsonl),
152	                "cwd": cwd,
153	                "first_ts": first_ts,
154	                "last_ts": last_ts,
155	                "prompts": prompts,
156	                "actions": actions,
157	            }
158	
159	_SESSION_CACHE = {"ts": None, "data": []}
160	
161	def get_claude_sessions():
162	    """Cheap in-process cache to avoid re-scanning JSONL on every refresh."""
163	    now = datetime.now()
164	    if _SESSION_CACHE["ts"] and (now - _SESSION_CACHE["ts"]).total_seconds() < 30:
165	        return _SESSION_CACHE["data"]
166	    data = list(_scan_claude_sessions())
167	    _SESSION_CACHE["ts"] = now
168	    _SESSION_CACHE["data"] = data
169	    return data
170	
171	def telemetry_for_brief(brief, sessions):
172	    """Aggregate sessions whose cwd contains or equals the brief's project_dir."""
173	    proj = Path(brief["project_dir"]).resolve()
174	    matched = []
175	    for s in sessions:
176	        if not s["cwd"]:
177	            continue
178	        try:
179	            cwd = Path(s["cwd"]).resolve()
180	        except OSError:
181	            continue
182	        try:
183	            proj.relative_to(cwd)
184	            matched.append(s)
185	            continue
186	        except ValueError:
187	            pass
188	        try:
189	            cwd.relative_to(proj)
190	            matched.append(s)
191	        except ValueError:
192	            pass
193	    if not matched:
194	        return {
195	            "sessions": 0,
196	            "prompts": 0,
197	            "actions": 0,
198	            "total_minutes": 0,
199	            "avg_session_min": None,
200	            "last_action_age_days": None,
201	            "last_touch": None,
202	            "last_touch_age_days": None,
203	        }
204	    total_min = 0.0
205	    durations = []
206	    last_action_ts = None
207	    last_touch_ts = None
208	    for s in matched:
209	        if s["first_ts"] and s["last_ts"]:
210	            d = (s["last_ts"] - s["first_ts"]).total_seconds() / 60
211	            total_min += d
212	            durations.append(d)
213	        if s["last_ts"] and (last_touch_ts is None or s["last_ts"] > last_touch_ts):
214	            last_touch_ts = s["last_ts"]
215	        if s["actions"] > 0 and s["last_ts"]:
216	            if last_action_ts is None or s["last_ts"] > last_action_ts:
217	                last_action_ts = s["last_ts"]
218	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
219	    return {
220	        "sessions": len(matched),
221	        "prompts": sum(s["prompts"] for s in matched),
222	        "actions": sum(s["actions"] for s in matched),
223	        "total_minutes": total_min,
224	        "avg_session_min": (sum(durations) / len(durations)) if durations else None,
225	        "last_action_age_days": ((now - last_action_ts).days if last_action_ts else None),
226	        "last_touch": (last_touch_ts.isoformat() if last_touch_ts else None),
227	        "last_touch_age_days": ((now - last_touch_ts).days if last_touch_ts else None),
228	    }
229	
230	def classify_pattern(tel):
231	    s = tel["sessions"]
232	    a = tel["actions"]
233	    p = tel["prompts"]
234	    avg = tel["avg_session_min"]
235	    age = tel["last_action_age_days"]
236	    touch_age = tel["last_touch_age_days"]
237	    if s == 0:
238	        return "fresh"
239	    if p >= 5 and a == 0:
240	        return "avoidance"
241	    if a == 0 and touch_age is not None and touch_age >= 7:
242	        return "stalled"
243	    if a > 0 and avg and avg >= 10 and touch_age is not None and touch_age <= 3:
244	        return "healthy"
245	    if a > 0:
246	        return "active"
247	    return "unknown"
248	
249	# ----- memory & priority -----
250	
251	_MEMORY_CACHE = {"ts": None, "keywords": []}
252	
253	def load_memory_keywords():
254	    now = datetime.now()
255	    if _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"] and (now - _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"]).total_seconds() < 60:
256	        return _MEMORY_CACHE["keywords"]
257	    memfile = CONFIG["memory_file"]
258	    if not memfile.exists():
259	        return []
260	    try:
261	        text = memfile.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
262	    except OSError:
263	        return []
264	    cjk = re.findall(r"[一-鿿]{2,}", text)
265	    latin = re.findall(r"\b[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{3,}\b", text)
266	    stop = {"the", "and", "with", "this", "that", "from", "have", "will",
267	            "name", "description", "metadata", "type", "memory", "file"}
268	    keywords = list({w for w in cjk + latin if w.lower() not in stop})
269	    _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"] = now
270	    _MEMORY_CACHE["keywords"] = keywords
271	    return keywords
272	
273	def evaluate_priority(brief, tel, memory_keywords):
274	    blob = " ".join([brief["name"], brief["what"], brief["next"]]).lower()
275	    score = 0.0
276	    reasons = []
277	    kw_hits = sum(1 for k in memory_keywords if k.lower() in blob)
278	    if kw_hits:
279	        bonus = min(kw_hits, 5) * 2
280	        score += bonus
281	        reasons.append(f"memory aligned ({kw_hits})")
282	    pattern = classify_pattern(tel)
283	    if pattern == "avoidance":
284	        score += 4
285	        reasons.append("avoidance signal — needs decision, not work")
286	    elif pattern == "stalled":
287	        score += 3
288	        reasons.append("stalled — needs unblock or kill")
289	    elif pattern == "fresh":
290	        score += 1
291	        reasons.append("fresh — no entries yet")
292	    elif pattern == "active":
293	        score += 0.5
294	    elif pattern == "healthy":
295	        score -= 1
296	        reasons.append("healthy — in flow, don't interrupt")
297	    if brief["status"] == "deferred":
298	        if brief["defer_until"]:
299	            reasons.append(f"deferred until {brief['defer_until']}")
300	        else:
301	            reasons.append("deferred without condition")
302	        score -= 2
303	    elif brief["status"] == "done":
304	        score = -100.0
305	        reasons.append("done")
306	    nxt = (brief["next"] or "").lower()
307	    if any(k in nxt for k in ["卡", "block", "stuck", "等"]):
308	        score += 1
309	        reasons.append("explicit blocker in next")
310	    return score, "; ".join(reasons) or "no signal", pattern
311	
312	# ----- spawn -----
313	
314	def _quote(s):
315	    return s.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"')
316	
317	def spawn_command(brief, vendor):
318	    project = brief["project_dir"]
319	    summary = brief["what"].strip()
320	    nxt = brief["next"].strip()
321	    parts = []
322	    if summary:
323	        parts.append(summary)
324	    if nxt:
325	        parts.append(f"\\n\\nNext: {nxt}")
326	    body = _quote("".join(parts))
327	    if vendor == "claude":
328	        return f'cd "{project}"\nclaude "{body}"'
329	    if vendor == "codex":
330	        return f'cd "{project}"\ncodex "{body}"'
331	    return ""
332	
333	# ----- flask -----
334	
335	app = Flask(__name__, template_folder=str(Path(__file__).parent / "templates"))
336	
337	@app.route("/")
338	def feed():
339	    briefs = scan_vault()
340	    sessions = get_claude_sessions()
341	    memory_keywords = load_memory_keywords()
342	    ranked = []
343	    for b in briefs:
344	        tel = telemetry_for_brief(b, sessions)
345	        score, reason, pattern = evaluate_priority(b, tel, memory_keywords)
346	        ranked.append({"brief": b, "tel": tel, "score": score,
347	                       "reason": reason, "pattern": pattern})
348	    ranked.sort(key=lambda r: -r["score"])
349	    counts = {}
350	    for r in ranked:
351	        counts[r["pattern"]] = counts.get(r["pattern"], 0) + 1
352	    return render_template("feed.html", ranked=ranked, counts=counts,
353	                           memory_keywords=len(memory_keywords))
354	
355	@app.route("/brief")
356	def brief_detail():
357	    path = request.args.get("path")
358	    if not path:
359	        return "missing path", 400
360	    p = Path(path)
361	    if not p.exists():
362	        return "not found", 404
363	    b = parse_brief(p)
364	    if not b:
365	        return "parse failed", 500
366	    sessions = get_claude_sessions()
367	    tel = telemetry_for_brief(b, sessions)
368	    score, reason, pattern = evaluate_priority(b, tel, load_memory_keywords())
369	    matched = []
370	    proj = Path(b["project_dir"]).resolve()
371	    for s in sessions:
372	        if not s["cwd"]:
373	            continue
374	        try:
375	            cwd = Path(s["cwd"]).resolve()
376	        except OSError:
377	            continue
378	        matches = False
379	        try:
380	            proj.relative_to(cwd)
381	            matches = True
382	        except ValueError:
383	            try:
384	                cwd.relative_to(proj)
385	                matches = True
386	            except ValueError:
387	                pass
388	        if matches:
389	            matched.append(s)
390	    matched.sort(key=lambda s: s["last_ts"] or datetime.min.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc), reverse=True)
391	    return render_template("detail.html", brief=b, telemetry=tel, pattern=pattern,
392	                           score=score, reason=reason, sessions=matched[:10],
393	                           spawn_claude=spawn_command(b, "claude"),
394	                           spawn_codex=spawn_command(b, "codex"))
395	
396	def main():
397	    print(f"attend — running at http://localhost:{CONFIG['port']}")
398	    print("vault roots:")
399	    for r in CONFIG["vault_roots"]:
400	        marker = "✓" if r.exists() else "✗"
401	        print(f"  {marker} {r}")
402	    print(f"claude projects: {CONFIG['claude_projects']} "
403	          f"({'✓' if CONFIG['claude_projects'].exists() else '✗'})")
404	    print(f"codex sessions: {CONFIG['codex_sessions']} "
405	          f"({'✓' if CONFIG['codex_sessions'].exists() else '✗ (skipped)'})")
406	    app.run(host="127.0.0.1", port=CONFIG["port"], debug=False)
407	
408	if __name__ == "__main__":
409	    main()
410

```md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Onboarding

`DESIGN.md` is the source of truth for *why* this exists — read it end-to-end before changing behavior. It covers the problem, the conceptual model, every rejected path (so you don't redo them), and the research touchstones behind the design. `brief.md` holds the current `next` step. `README.md` has the user-facing contract.

## Commands

\```powershell
pip install -r requirements.txt
python daemon.py          # serves http://localhost:5050
\```

There is no test suite, linter, or build step — this is a single ~400-line Flask script. Verify changes by running the daemon and loading the page.

## Architecture

The entire backend is `daemon.py`; `templates/feed.html` (ranked list) and `templates/detail.html` (per-brief + spawn commands) are the only views. The pipeline, in order, all in `daemon.py`:

1. **`scan_vault()`** — `rglob("brief.md")` across `CONFIG["vault_roots"]`. Each brief is a markdown file with YAML front matter (`status`, `defer_until`, `last_touch`) plus `## what` / `## accept` / `## next` sections. Only those three sections are parsed; others are ignored. The brief lives *at the task's project dir*, not in a central folder — `project_dir = path.parent` is the matching key.
2. **`_scan_claude_sessions()`** — parses Claude Code JSONL transcripts under `~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl`, extracting `cwd`, timestamps, prompt count, and action count (tool_use of `Edit`/`Write`/`NotebookEdit`/`Bash`/`PowerShell`). Cached in-process for 30 s (`_SESSION_CACHE`). Codex JSONL is **not parsed in v0** (schema unknown).
3. **`telemetry_for_brief()`** — matches sessions to a brief by **cwd containment** in either direction (session cwd inside project dir, or vice versa), then aggregates sessions/prompts/actions/dwell/last-touch.
4. **`classify_pattern()`** — maps telemetry to `fresh` / `avoidance` / `stalled` / `healthy` / `active`. See the table in `DESIGN.md` / `README.md` for exact thresholds.
5. **`evaluate_priority()`** — `memory-keyword alignment ×2 + pattern weight + explicit-blocker bonus − defer/done penalty`, returning a score, a composed human-readable reason, and the pattern. Memory keywords come from `CONFIG["memory_file"]` (the user's MEMORY.md), CJK + latin tokens, cached 60 s. **Time is deliberately not a priority input** (see DESIGN.md "Rejected paths").
6. **`spawn_command()`** — emits copy-paste `cd … && claude/codex "<brief>"`. v0 never auto-spawns; it only renders commands to copy.

Vendor-specific logic is confined to exactly two seams: JSONL ingestion (step 2) and spawn command emission (step 6). Everything between is vendor-neutral.

All paths/ports live in the `CONFIG` dict at the top of `daemon.py` — there is no separate config file.

## Design invariants (do not violate without explicit cause)

1. **brief = state, session = cache** — nothing the dashboard knows may live only in a session.
2. **pull, not push** — no notifications/SSE/websockets.
3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** — pattern labels and reason strings must be observation-form ("5 prompts, 0 actions"), never second-person pressure ("you again", "still stuck"). This is the Steel 2007 constraint; making output more "motivating" is a regression, not an improvement.
4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** — keep vendor logic inside the two seams above.
5. **single polling surface** — adding a second dashboard/tab/tool is a regression. Check new organizational layers against this.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-05-30 13:27:53Z)**_

Created `CLAUDE.md`. Since this codebase already has unusually strong design docs, I kept it lean and oriented around what's *not* obvious from a single file:

- **Onboarding pointer** — `DESIGN.md` is the real source of truth (especially its "rejected paths" so future sessions don't redo dead ends); CLAUDE.md just routes there rather than duplicating it.
- **Commands** — `pip install -r requirements.txt` + `python daemon.py`. I noted explicitly that there's no test/lint/build step, so future instances don't go hunting for one.
- **Architecture** — the 6-stage pipeline through `daemon.py` (scan → ingest JSONL → cwd-match → classify → score → spawn), and the key insight that requires reading multiple files: vendor-specific code is confined to exactly two seams (JSONL parsing, spawn emission), everything else is vendor-neutral.
- **Invariants** — the 5 hard rules, with emphasis on #3 (descriptive-not-judgmental telemetry), since that's a constraint a well-meaning future edit would most easily break.

One thing worth flagging: the `Bash` tool reported this directory as not a git repo (and the environment says so too), but `python daemon.py` and the matching logic point at real paths like `~/.claude/projects` and `D:/workspace/projects`. If you do plan to version this, `.specstory/` is present but there's no `.gitignore` yet.

83	    font-size: 0.9rem;
84	    margin: 0.3rem 0;
85	    padding: 0.35rem 0.6rem;
86	    background: rgba(0,0,0,0.04);
87	    border-radius: 3px;
88	  }
89	  .next::before { content: "next · "; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.75rem; }
90	  .meta {
91	    font-size: 0.75rem;
92	    color: #6b7280;
93	    margin-top: 0.4rem;
94	    font-family: ui-monospace, "Cascadia Mono", monospace;
95	  }
96	  .reason { color: #4b5563; font-style: italic; }
97	  .empty {
98	    color: #9ca3af;
99	    padding: 3rem 1rem;
100	    text-align: center;
101	    border: 1px dashed #d1d5db;
102	    background: white;
103	    border-radius: 6px;
104	  }
105	  .empty code { background: #f3f4f6; padding: 0.1rem 0.3rem; border-radius: 3px; }
106	</style>
107	</head>
108	<body>
109	<div class="topbar">
110	  <h1>attend <span class="accent">{{ ranked|length }} briefs · memory keywords: {{ memory_keywords }}</span></h1>
111	  <button onclick="location.reload()">refresh</button>
112	</div>
113	
114	<div class="pill-row">
115	  {% for pat in ["avoidance", "stalled", "active", "fresh", "healthy", "unknown", "done"] %}
116	    {% if counts.get(pat) %}<span>{{ pat }}: {{ counts[pat] }}</span>{% endif %}
117	  {% endfor %}
118	</div>
119	
120	{% if not ranked %}
121	  <div class="empty">
122	    No <code>brief.md</code> found in any vault root.<br>
123	    Create one at <code>projects/&lt;name&gt;/brief.md</code> to start.
124	  </div>
125	{% endif %}
126	
127	{% for r in ranked %}
128	{% set b = r.brief %}
129	{% set t = r.tel %}
130	<div class="brief {{ r.pattern }}{% if b.status == 'done' %} done{% endif %}">
131	  <div class="title">
132	    <a href="/brief?path={{ b.path|urlencode }}">{{ b.name }}</a>
133	    <span class="badges">
134	      <span class="pattern">{{ r.pattern }}</span>
135	      score {{ "%.1f"|format(r.score) }}
136	    </span>
137	  </div>
138	  {% if b.what %}<div class="what">{{ b.what }}</div>{% endif %}
139	  {% if b.next %}<div class="next">{{ b.next }}</div>{% endif %}
140	  <div class="meta">
141	    {% if t.sessions %}
142	      {{ t.sessions }} sessions · {{ t.prompts }} prompts · {{ t.actions }} actions
143	      {% if t.avg_session_min %} · avg {{ "%.0f"|format(t.avg_session_min) }}m {% endif %}
144	      {% if t.last_touch_age_days is not none %} · touched {{ t.last_touch_age_days }}d ago {% endif %}
145	    {% else %}
146	      no sessions matched
147	    {% endif %}
148	    · <span class="reason">{{ r.reason }}</span>
149	  </div>
150	</div>
151	{% endfor %}
152	
153	</body>
154	</html>
155

174	function copyCmd(id) {
175	  const text = document.getElementById(id).textContent;
176	  navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
177	    const btns = document.querySelectorAll("button");
178	    btns.forEach(b => {
179	      if (b.getAttribute("onclick") === `copyCmd('${id}')`) {
180	        const orig = b.textContent;
181	        b.textContent = "copied ✓";
182	        setTimeout(() => { b.textContent = orig; }, 1200);
183	      }
184	    });
185	  });
186	}
187	</script>

I've now read the entire codebase (it's compact: `daemon.py` plus two templates and the design docs, all of which I've fully read). I don't need to spawn Explore agents to re-derive what's already in context. The design philosophy and your taste come through very clearly in `DESIGN.md`:

- **Aggressive minimalism** — single ~400-line file, rejected the desktop app / TUI / heavy PKM precisely because of "engineering overhead without benefit."
- **Conceptual invariants over features** — brief=state/session=cache, pull-not-push, descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007), vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution, single polling surface.
- **Research-grounded, bilingual, concise.**

**现在是单文件 daemon.py (Flask, 路径硬编码到 D:/workspace 和 OneDrive)。要把它做成扩展友好+跨平台,走哪条路?**

Options:
- **原地重构 Python (推荐)** - 保留 Flask + 极简依赖,把 daemon.py 拆成包:config 层 / vendor adapter (claude+codex JSONL 统一接口) / telemetry+priority core / web 层。最贴合 DESIGN.md 的极简 taste,扩展点= adapter 接口。
- **重写为 FastAPI + 类型化 core** - async + pydantic 模型 + 更现代结构。更‘正统’但依赖更重,偏离单文件极简。
- **前后端都换 (TS/React 等)** - 最重,引入前端构建链。与现有 taste 冲突最大,我不推荐。

**'create team 自动迭代直到完美' — Claude Code 没有常驻 team,且 400 行项目让多个冷启动 agent 反复读文件并不划算。你希望我怎么推进?**

Options:
- **里程碑 + 检查点 (推荐)** - 我分阶段做 (config化→adapter→测试→跨平台→打磨),每个里程碑跑测试并停下来让你 review 再继续。我自己做主体,只在 review 环节用子 agent 做独立评审。
- **自主跑到既定验收标准** - 先和你定一份书面验收 bar + 测试全绿,我不中途停,达标后一次性汇报。风险:'完美'不可控,可能偏离你的细节偏好。
- **一次一个 PR 规模改动** - 每次只做一个内聚改动就停下 review,最稳但最慢。

**核心诉求是‘可指定目录’+像 Claude 那样 per-project memory。配置与 memory 模型怎么定?**

Options:
- **配置文件 + 自动发现 (推荐)** - attend.toml 配 vault roots / vendor session 目录 / memory 目录;CLI flag 和环境变量可覆盖;平台默认值自动按 Win/macOS 选。memory 既支持全局 MEMORY.md 也支持 per-project (从 brief 向上找 .claude/ 或项目内 memory)。
- **纯自动发现,零配置** - 全部靠约定:从 brief 所在目录向上找 vendor 目录和 memory。最省心但可控性差。
- **纯配置文件,不自动发现** - 一切显式写在配置里,行为最可预测但接入新项目要手动加。

**你要求 Win + macOS 都能跑。我这台是 Windows,macOS 怎么验证?**

Options:
- **我跨平台编码+单测, 你在 Mac 跑 (推荐)** - 用 pathlib/平台抽象写代码,补 pytest 覆盖路径与 adapter 逻辑;我在 Windows 跑全套,你在 Mac 上跑一次冒烟验证。
- **加 GitHub Actions win+macos 矩阵** - CI 自动跑两平台测试。注意:此目录当前不是 git 仓库,需要先 git init + 建远端。
- **只靠路径抽象+单测,不实跑 Mac** - 信任 pathlib 跨平台,不做真实 macOS 运行验证。最快但留风险。


**Answer:** 最终应该用tauri, 自主跑到既定验收标准, tauri架构, tauri架构
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-05-30 13:37:10Z)**_

That's a significant pivot — Tauri means a native cross-platform app (Rust core + web UI), which is a deliberate reversal of two things you previously wrote into `DESIGN.md` ("desktop app — engineering overhead without benefit", and localhost-tab as the single polling surface). I'm fine respecting that override since you own the design — Tauri actually *keeps* the other invariants intact (single surface, pull-not-push, descriptive telemetry, vendor-neutral data), it just changes the shell from a browser tab to a native window and solves cross-platform distribution + native folder-pickers for "指定目录" cleanly.

Before I write the acceptance bar and plan, let me check what's actually installed on this machine (read-only), because the Tauri toolchain availability shapes the plan.

```md
# attend → Tauri 跨平台重构

## Context

`attend` 现在是单文件 `daemon.py` (Flask, ~400 行) + 两个 HTML 模板。路径硬编码到 `D:/workspace/projects`、OneDrive、`~/.claude`,只在 Windows 上跑,Codex 未解析,无测试,无配置层,memory 指向单个写死的 `MEMORY.md`。

用户决定 **pivot 到 Tauri**:把 attend 做成 Win/macOS 原生跨平台 app,扩展友好、架构优雅、带自动测试。这是对 `DESIGN.md` 两条旧记录的有意推翻("desktop app — 工程开销无收益"、"localhost tab 作为 single polling surface")。其余 invariant 全部保留:**pull-not-push、descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007)、vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution、single polling surface**(原生窗口即那个唯一面)。

工作模式:**自主跑到下方既定验收标准**,不中途停;遇阻塞或完成时汇报。

**Memory 模型不变** —— 完全沿用 Claude Code 的方式(per-project memory,按 Claude 现有约定发现与读取)。不引入新的 memory 抽象,只把当前"单个全局 MEMORY.md"泛化为 per-project 发现。

## 目标架构

\```
attend/
├── crates/
│   └── attend-core/          # 纯 Rust 领域逻辑, 零 Tauri 依赖, 可 cargo test
│       ├── config.rs         # 配置 + 平台默认 (dirs crate)
│       ├── brief.rs          # brief.md 解析 (frontmatter + what/accept/next)
│       ├── vendor/           # 扩展点: SessionSource trait
│       │   ├── mod.rs        #   trait SessionSource { fn sessions() -> Vec<Session> }
│       │   ├── claude.rs     #   Claude JSONL 解析 (port 自 daemon.py)
│       │   └── codex.rs      #   Codex 解析 or 干净 stub
│       ├── telemetry.rs      # cwd 包含匹配 + 聚合
│       ├── pattern.rs        # fresh/avoidance/stalled/healthy/active 分类
│       ├── memory.rs         # Claude-约定 per-project memory 关键词提取
│       └── priority.rs       # score + 一行 reason
├── src-tauri/                # Tauri 2 app: 薄 command 层, 调 attend-core
│   ├── src/main.rs / lib.rs  # #[tauri::command] feed() / brief_detail() / config CRUD / pick_dir()
│   └── tauri.conf.json
├── ui/                       # Vite + 原生 TS (无 React, 复用现有 HTML/CSS)
│   ├── index.html  feed.ts  detail.ts  styles.css
└── legacy/daemon.py          # 旧实现保留作参考 (不删)
\```

**关键设计模式**
- `attend-core` 与 Tauri 解耦 → 逻辑可单测,Tauri 只做 IPC 桥。
- `SessionSource` trait = vendor 扩展点(新 vendor = 新 impl,呼应 invariant 4)。
- 前端走 **vanilla TS + Vite**,直接复用现有 `feed.html`/`detail.html` 的 CSS,不引前端框架重量(呼应极简 taste)。
- Tauri 2.x;包管理用 pnpm。

## 既定验收标准 (= "perfect")

1. `cargo tauri dev` / `build` 在 Windows 成功,弹出原生窗口。
2. **功能对齐现 daemon**:扫 vault roots → 解析 brief → Claude JSONL telemetry(sessions/prompts/actions/dwell/last_touch)→ pattern 分类 → priority+reason → feed 列表 + detail 视图 → spawn 命令复制(Tauri clipboard API)。
3. **可指定目录**:vault roots / vendor session 目录 可在 app 内用原生文件夹选择器设定并持久化;Win/macOS 平台默认值自动填充。
4. **Memory**:per-project,沿用 Claude 约定;关键词进 priority。行为与现状一致,仅泛化到 per-project。
5. **Codex adapter**:trait 就位;若能从样本推出 schema 则给可用 parser,否则给干净 stub(绝不造假数据)。
6. **自动测试**:`attend-core` 有 `cargo test` 覆盖 brief 解析 / cwd 匹配(含 `\` 与 `/` 分隔符)/ pattern 阈值 / priority 打分 / 平台路径默认。`cargo test` 与 `cargo clippy` 干净。
7. **跨平台**:无硬编码路径,全用 `PathBuf` + `dirs` crate 平台默认。我在 Windows 跑全套;你在 Mac 跑一次 `cargo tauri dev` 冒烟。
8. **invariant 保留**:descriptive telemetry 字符串无第二人称施压词;pull-not-push;core vendor-neutral;单一面。
9. **文档**:`DESIGN.md` 加 "v1: Tauri pivot" 段(记录 invariant 变更与理由);`README.md` 换 Tauri 运行说明;`CLAUDE.md` 更新为新结构。

## 执行里程碑 (自主连续推进)

- **M0** `git init`(给大改一个安全网/可回滚);`cargo install tauri-cli`;`pnpm` 装前端依赖。
- **M1** Scaffold:workspace(`crates/attend-core` + `src-tauri`)+ Vite/TS `ui/` 骨架。
- **M2** Port 领域逻辑到 `attend-core` + 单测(config/brief/vendor trait+claude/telemetry/pattern/memory/priority)。
- **M3** Tauri command 层 + IPC;移植 feed + detail UI(复用现有 HTML/CSS)。
- **M4** 配置 UI(文件夹选择器、持久化)+ spawn 命令复制。
- **M5** Codex adapter(parser 或干净 stub)+ 跨平台路径测试。
- **M6** Polish + clippy + `cargo test` + Windows `cargo tauri dev` 截图验证 + 更新三份文档;移 `daemon.py` 进 `legacy/`。

## 关键移植参考 (daemon.py → Rust)

- `parse_brief` (L33–73) → `brief.rs`:`serde_yaml` 解 frontmatter,正则 `^#+\s+(\S+)` 切 section。
- `_scan_claude_sessions` (L101–157) → `vendor/claude.rs`:逐行 JSON,取 `cwd`/`timestamp`/`type`,action = tool_use ∈ {Edit,Write,NotebookEdit,Bash,PowerShell}。
- `telemetry_for_brief` (L171–228) → `telemetry.rs`:`proj.relative_to(cwd)` 双向包含 → `PathBuf` 前缀判断。
- `classify_pattern` (L230–247) / `evaluate_priority` (L273–310) → `pattern.rs` / `priority.rs`,阈值与权重 1:1 搬。
- `load_memory_keywords` (L253–271) → `memory.rs`:CJK `[一-鿿]{2,}` + latin,泛化为 per-project。
- `spawn_command` (L317–331) → command 层。

## 验证

- `cd crates/attend-core && cargo test` 全绿;`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` 无告警。
- `cargo tauri dev` 启动原生窗口;feed 显示已有 brief(本仓库 `brief.md` + 其他 vault),detail 页 telemetry/spawn 正常,文件夹选择器改 vault root 后刷新生效。
- 我在 Windows 完成上述并截图;移交后你在 macOS 跑 `cargo tauri dev` 冒烟。

## 风险 / 备注

- 与旧 `DESIGN.md` invariant 冲突已确认是有意 pivot,会在文档显式记录,不算违规。
- `cargo install tauri-cli` 与首次 `cargo build` 可能耗时数分钟(Rust 编译)。
- 全程在 Windows 验证;macOS 真机冒烟需你执行。
- Codex schema 未知时只给 stub,不伪造数据(invariant 3/4)。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-05-30 16:55:16Z)**_

Good catch — that question exposes that **Tauri is the wrong tool for what you actually want.**

Tauri builds a **native desktop app**: it bundles a Rust binary + the OS WebView into a platform-specific installer (`.msi` / `.dmg` / `.AppImage`). The user *downloads and installs an app*. There's no "open in any browser via localhost" mode — the UI renders into an embedded window, not a public local server. So Tauri is the opposite of "no download."

What you're describing — **start a local process, open in the browser, run via `npx` from a remote repo, zero install** — is the classic **Node CLI that boots a local web server** pattern (`npx serve`, `npx http-server`, `npx vite`). You publish to npm; `npx attend` downloads-and-runs on demand, no permanent install, works on Win/macOS because Node does. This is actually *much closer to your original `DESIGN.md`* (localhost single surface, pull-not-push) than Tauri was — the only real change from today's Flask app is **Python → Node/TS**, so that `npx` distribution works.

That said, there's one fork worth locking before I rewrite the plan, since it's the biggest cost driver:

**零安装 + 本地起进程 + 浏览器打开 + 远程仓库跑 — 采用哪种 runtime/分发?**

Options:
- **Node + TypeScript → npx (推荐)** - 发到 npm,`npx attend [目录]` 直接跑,起本地 server + 自动开浏览器。真零安装,跨平台,一种语言写 server+UI+测试(vitest)。代价:现有 Python 逻辑要移植到 TS。最贴合你说的 'npx 远程仓库'。
- **保留 Python → uvx** - 发到 PyPI,`uvx attend [目录]` 跑(需用户装了 uv)。同样无常驻安装,移植成本最低(逻辑不动)。但不是 npx,且要求用户有 uv 而非更通用的 node。
- **Node 启动器 + Python sidecar** - npx 起 Node, 背后调 Python 逻辑。要同时装 Node 和 Python,最复杂,不推荐。


**Answer:** npx更好, 但是这种远程是否要发版到npm仓库, 这是什么成本, 解释npm npx node
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-05-30 17:08:13Z)**_

Let me answer your npm/npx/node question directly, then I'll rewrite the plan around it.

## node / npm / npx — what they are

- **node** — the JavaScript runtime (think: the Python interpreter, but for JS/TS). Installed once per machine; it's what actually *runs* the local server process. Exists on both Win and macOS.
- **npm** — Node's package manager. It's two things: ① a CLI like `pip`, and ② the public registry at `registry.npmjs.org` where packages are published and downloaded.
- **npx** — a runner bundled with npm that executes a package's command **without permanently installing it**: fetch → run → done. The only question is *where* `npx <thing>` resolves the package from.

## Does "npx 远程" require publishing to npm? — No. Three options, cost descending:

1. **Publish to npm registry** → `npx attend`
   - Cost: free npm account, claim an unused name (or a scope like `@sin/attend`), `npm publish` per release. **Public packages are free** (private ones cost). Cleanest command, anyone can run it.
   - Downsides: code is public; every release is `npm version` + `npm publish`; name-squatting.
2. **npx straight from GitHub** → `npx github:you/attend` — **no npm publish at all**
   - Cost: ~zero. Just `git push`. No npm account. Works with private repos (needs a token).
   - Downsides: longer command; needs a build step (`prepare`) or committed `dist/`.
3. **No remote** → `git clone` once → `npm install` → `npm start`
   - You have two of your own machines (Win + Mac); cloning once is trivial. The *only* thing npx-from-remote buys you is running on a brand-new machine without cloning.

**The key insight:** whether you ever `npm publish` does **not** change how I build it. If I make it a standard npm package (a `bin` entry + a `dist/` build), then **all three** work automatically — you can `npx github:you/attend` today and decide on `npm publish` later, or never. So I'll build for that superset and leave publishing as a final, optional one-liner.

I've rewritten the plan around **Node + TypeScript, npx-distributable local web app** (which also restores your original `DESIGN.md` localhost/single-surface invariant that Tauri would have broken).

# attend → Node/TS npx-distributable 本地 web app

## Context

`attend` 现在是单文件 `daemon.py` (Flask, ~400 行) + 两个 HTML 模板:路径硬编码、仅 Windows、Codex 未解析、无测试、无配置层、memory 指向写死的单个 `MEMORY.md`。

用户目标(经多轮澄清):**零安装、本地起进程、浏览器打开、可 `npx` 从远程仓库跑**,Win/macOS 都能用。Tauri 被否决(那是"下载安装原生 app",与"免下载"相反)。正确形态 = **Node CLI 启本地 web server**(`npx serve` 那类模式)。这其实**恢复**了原 `DESIGN.md` 的 localhost / single-surface invariant —— 仅把 runtime 从 Python 换成 Node/TS,以支持 `npx` 分发。

保留的 invariant:**pull-not-push、descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007)、vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution、single polling surface(localhost 那一页)**。

**Memory 模型不变** —— 完全沿用 Claude Code 的 per-project memory 约定,只把当前"单个全局 MEMORY.md"泛化为按项目发现。不引入新 memory 抽象。

**可指定目录** —— 通过 CLI 参数 / 配置文件 / 环境变量指定 vault roots 与 vendor session 目录;平台默认值自动按 Win/macOS 填。

**分发**:做成标准 npm 包(`bin` 入口 + `dist/` 构建),则 `npx github:you/attend`、`npm publish` 后 `npx attend`、本地 `npm start` 三种方式全自动支持。**是否 `npm publish` 留到最后一步再决定,不影响实现。**

工作模式:**自主跑到下方既定验收标准**,不中途停;遇阻塞或完成时汇报。

## 目标架构

\```
attend/
├── package.json            # bin: { attend: "dist/cli.js" }, scripts, deps
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts              # node:util parseArgs (指定目录/--port), 启 server, 开浏览器
│   ├── server.ts           # Hono 本地 server: 渲染 feed/detail + 静态资源
│   ├── config.ts           # CLI flags + 配置文件 + env, 平台默认 (os.homedir)
│   ├── core/               # 纯逻辑, 零 server 依赖, 可单测
│   │   ├── brief.ts        #   解析 brief.md (frontmatter + what/accept/next)
│   │   ├── vendor/
│   │   │   ├── index.ts    #   interface SessionSource (扩展点)
│   │   │   ├── claude.ts   #   Claude JSONL 解析 (port daemon.py)
│   │   │   └── codex.ts    #   Codex 解析 or 干净 stub
│   │   ├── telemetry.ts    #   cwd 包含匹配 + 聚合
│   │   ├── pattern.ts      #   fresh/avoidance/stalled/healthy/active
│   │   ├── memory.ts       #   Claude-约定 per-project memory 关键词
│   │   └── priority.ts     #   score + 一行 reason
│   └── ui/                 # 复用现有 feed.html/detail.html 的 HTML+CSS
├── test/                   # vitest 单测
└── legacy/daemon.py        # 旧实现保留作参考 (不删)
\```

**关键设计模式**
- `core/` 与 server 解耦 → 逻辑可 vitest 单测,server 只做 HTTP/渲染。
- `SessionSource` interface = vendor 扩展点(新 vendor = 新实现,呼应 invariant 4)。
- **server-rendered HTML**(沿用现 Flask 思路),不引前端框架;直接移植现有两个模板的 HTML/CSS,保留 refresh 按钮(pull-not-push)。
- HTTP 用 **Hono**(极小、现代、优雅);其余尽量零依赖(CLI 参数用内置 `node:util` parseArgs,开浏览器用轻量 `open`)。
- TS 严格模式;lint 用 biome(单一工具,快)。

## 既定验收标准 (= "perfect")

1. `npm install && npm run build` 干净;`node dist/cli.js` 与 `npx .` 启 server 并自动开浏览器到 `localhost:5050`。
2. **功能对齐现 daemon**:扫 vault roots → 解析 brief → Claude JSONL telemetry → pattern 分类 → priority+reason → feed 列表 + detail 视图 → spawn 命令复制。
3. **可指定目录**:`attend [dir...]` / `--port` / 配置文件 / env 均可设 vault roots 与 session 目录;Win/macOS 默认值自动。
4. **Memory**:per-project,沿用 Claude 约定;关键词进 priority;行为与现状一致仅泛化。
5. **Codex adapter**:interface 就位;能从样本推出 schema 则给可用 parser,否则干净 stub(绝不造假数据)。
6. **自动测试**:vitest 覆盖 brief 解析 / cwd 匹配(含 `\` 与 `/`)/ pattern 阈值 / priority 打分 / 平台路径默认;`tsc --noEmit` 与 biome lint 干净。
7. **跨平台**:无硬编码路径,全用 `node:path` + `os.homedir()`。我在 Windows 跑全套;你在 Mac `npx` 冒烟。
8. **invariant 保留**:telemetry 字符串无第二人称施压词;pull-not-push;core vendor-neutral;单一面。
9. **分发就绪**:`bin` + build 到位,`npx github:you/attend` 可跑;`npm publish` 作为可选最后一步(README 写清两条命令)。
10. **文档**:`DESIGN.md` 加 "v1: Node/npx" 段(记录 Python→Node 的理由 + 否决 Tauri + 仍守 localhost invariant);`README.md` 换 npx 运行说明;`CLAUDE.md` 更新结构。

## 执行里程碑 (自主连续推进)

- **M0** `git init`(安全网);`npm init` + tsconfig + 依赖(typescript, hono, vitest, tsx, open, @biomejs/biome)。
- **M1** Scaffold:package + `cli.ts` + `server.ts` 骨架,先服务一个 hello 页跑通 `node dist/cli.js`。
- **M2** Port `core/` 到 TS + vitest(brief / vendor interface+claude / telemetry / pattern / memory / priority / config 平台默认)。
- **M3** server 路由 feed + detail,移植现有 HTML/CSS 模板。
- **M4** config(CLI 参数/文件/env)+ 指定目录 + 自动开浏览器 + spawn 复制。
- **M5** Codex adapter(parser 或干净 stub)+ 跨平台路径测试。
- **M6** Polish + biome + `tsc` + vitest + Windows `node dist/cli.js` 截图验证 + 更新三份文档 + 移 `daemon.py` 进 `legacy/`。

## 关键移植参考 (daemon.py → TS)

- `parse_brief` (L33–73) → `brief.ts`:frontmatter(`yaml` 或手写)+ 正则 `^#+\s+(\S+)` 切 section。
- `_scan_claude_sessions` (L101–157) → `vendor/claude.ts`:逐行 JSON,取 `cwd`/`timestamp`/`type`,action = tool_use ∈ {Edit,Write,NotebookEdit,Bash,PowerShell}。

- `load_memory_keywords` (L253–271) → `memory.ts`:CJK `[一-鿿]{2,}` + latin,泛化为 per-project。
- `spawn_command` (L317–331) + Flask 路由 (L337–394) → `server.ts`。

- `node dist/cli.js D:\workspace\projects` 启动并自动开浏览器;feed 显示本仓库 `brief.md` 等,detail 页 telemetry/spawn 正常;`--port`/指定目录 生效。

- 我在 Windows 完成上述并截图;移交后你在 macOS `npx github:you/attend`(或 `npm start`)冒烟。

## 风险 / 备注

## Context

`attend` 现在是单文件 `daemon.py` (Flask, ~400 行) + 两个 HTML 模板:路径硬编码、仅 Windows、Codex 未解析、无测试、无配置层、memory 指向写死的单个 `MEMORY.md`。

用户目标(经多轮澄清):**零安装、本地起进程、浏览器打开、可 `npx` 从远程仓库跑**,Win/macOS 都能用。Tauri 被否决(那是"下载安装原生 app",与"免下载"相反)。正确形态 = **Node CLI 启本地 web server**(`npx serve` 那类模式)。这其实**恢复**了原 `DESIGN.md` 的 localhost / single-surface invariant —— 仅把 runtime 从 Python 换成 Node/TS,以支持 `npx` 分发。

保留的 invariant:**pull-not-push、descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007)、vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution、single polling surface(localhost 那一页)**。

**Memory 模型不变** —— 完全沿用 Claude Code 的 per-project memory 约定,只把当前"单个全局 MEMORY.md"泛化为按项目发现。不引入新 memory 抽象。

**可指定目录** —— 通过 CLI 参数 / 配置文件 / 环境变量指定 vault roots 与 vendor session 目录;平台默认值自动按 Win/macOS 填。

**分发**:做成标准 npm 包(`bin` 入口 + `dist/` 构建),则 `npx github:you/attend`、`npm publish` 后 `npx attend`、本地 `npm start` 三种方式全自动支持。**是否 `npm publish` 留到最后一步再决定,不影响实现。**

工作模式:**自主跑到下方既定验收标准**,不中途停;遇阻塞或完成时汇报。

## 目标架构

```
attend/
├── package.json            # bin: { attend: "dist/cli.js" }, scripts, deps
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts              # node:util parseArgs (指定目录/--port), 启 server, 开浏览器
│   ├── server.ts           # Hono 本地 server: 渲染 feed/detail + 静态资源
│   ├── config.ts           # CLI flags + 配置文件 + env, 平台默认 (os.homedir)
│   ├── core/               # 纯逻辑, 零 server 依赖, 可单测
│   │   ├── brief.ts        #   解析 brief.md (frontmatter + what/accept/next)
│   │   ├── vendor/
│   │   │   ├── index.ts    #   interface SessionSource (扩展点)
│   │   │   ├── claude.ts   #   Claude JSONL 解析 (port daemon.py)
│   │   │   └── codex.ts    #   Codex 解析 or 干净 stub
│   │   ├── telemetry.ts    #   cwd 包含匹配 + 聚合
│   │   ├── pattern.ts      #   fresh/avoidance/stalled/healthy/active
│   │   ├── memory.ts       #   Claude-约定 per-project memory 关键词
│   │   └── priority.ts     #   score + 一行 reason
│   └── ui/                 # 复用现有 feed.html/detail.html 的 HTML+CSS
├── test/                   # vitest 单测
└── legacy/daemon.py        # 旧实现保留作参考 (不删)
```

**关键设计模式**
- `core/` 与 server 解耦 → 逻辑可 vitest 单测,server 只做 HTTP/渲染。
- `SessionSource` interface = vendor 扩展点(新 vendor = 新实现,呼应 invariant 4)。
- **server-rendered HTML**(沿用现 Flask 思路),不引前端框架;直接移植现有两个模板的 HTML/CSS,保留 refresh 按钮(pull-not-push)。
- HTTP 用 **Hono**(极小、现代、优雅);其余尽量零依赖(CLI 参数用内置 `node:util` parseArgs,开浏览器用轻量 `open`)。
- TS 严格模式;lint 用 biome(单一工具,快)。

## 既定验收标准 (= "perfect")

1. `npm install && npm run build` 干净;`node dist/cli.js` 与 `npx .` 启 server 并自动开浏览器到 `localhost:5050`。
2. **功能对齐现 daemon**:扫 vault roots → 解析 brief → Claude JSONL telemetry → pattern 分类 → priority+reason → feed 列表 + detail 视图 → spawn 命令复制。
3. **可指定目录**:`attend [dir...]` / `--port` / 配置文件 / env 均可设 vault roots 与 session 目录;Win/macOS 默认值自动。
4. **Memory**:per-project,沿用 Claude 约定;关键词进 priority;行为与现状一致仅泛化。
5. **Codex adapter**:interface 就位;能从样本推出 schema 则给可用 parser,否则干净 stub(绝不造假数据)。
6. **自动测试**:vitest 覆盖 brief 解析 / cwd 匹配(含 `\` 与 `/`)/ pattern 阈值 / priority 打分 / 平台路径默认;`tsc --noEmit` 与 biome lint 干净。
7. **跨平台**:无硬编码路径,全用 `node:path` + `os.homedir()`。我在 Windows 跑全套;你在 Mac `npx` 冒烟。
8. **invariant 保留**:telemetry 字符串无第二人称施压词;pull-not-push;core vendor-neutral;单一面。
9. **分发就绪**:`bin` + build 到位,`npx github:you/attend` 可跑;`npm publish` 作为可选最后一步(README 写清两条命令)。
10. **文档**:`DESIGN.md` 加 "v1: Node/npx" 段(记录 Python→Node 的理由 + 否决 Tauri + 仍守 localhost invariant);`README.md` 换 npx 运行说明;`CLAUDE.md` 更新结构。

## 执行里程碑 (自主连续推进)

- **M0** `git init`(安全网);`npm init` + tsconfig + 依赖(typescript, hono, vitest, tsx, open, @biomejs/biome)。
- **M1** Scaffold:package + `cli.ts` + `server.ts` 骨架,先服务一个 hello 页跑通 `node dist/cli.js`。
- **M2** Port `core/` 到 TS + vitest(brief / vendor interface+claude / telemetry / pattern / memory / priority / config 平台默认)。
- **M3** server 路由 feed + detail,移植现有 HTML/CSS 模板。
- **M4** config(CLI 参数/文件/env)+ 指定目录 + 自动开浏览器 + spawn 复制。
- **M5** Codex adapter(parser 或干净 stub)+ 跨平台路径测试。
- **M6** Polish + biome + `tsc` + vitest + Windows `node dist/cli.js` 截图验证 + 更新三份文档 + 移 `daemon.py` 进 `legacy/`。

## 关键移植参考 (daemon.py → TS)

- `parse_brief` (L33–73) → `brief.ts`:frontmatter(`yaml` 或手写)+ 正则 `^#+\s+(\S+)` 切 section。
- `_scan_claude_sessions` (L101–157) → `vendor/claude.ts`:逐行 JSON,取 `cwd`/`timestamp`/`type`,action = tool_use ∈ {Edit,Write,NotebookEdit,Bash,PowerShell}。
- `telemetry_for_brief` (L171–228) → `telemetry.ts`:双向 cwd 包含 → `path` 前缀判断(规范化分隔符)。
- `classify_pattern` (L230–247) / `evaluate_priority` (L273–310) → `pattern.ts` / `priority.ts`,阈值权重 1:1 搬。
- `load_memory_keywords` (L253–271) → `memory.ts`:CJK `[一-鿿]{2,}` + latin,泛化为 per-project。
- `spawn_command` (L317–331) + Flask 路由 (L337–394) → `server.ts`。

## 验证

- `npm test` (vitest) 全绿;`npx tsc --noEmit` 与 `npx biome check` 无告警。
- `node dist/cli.js D:\workspace\projects` 启动并自动开浏览器;feed 显示本仓库 `brief.md` 等,detail 页 telemetry/spawn 正常;`--port`/指定目录 生效。
- `npx .` 在仓库根可跑(验证 bin/打包正确)。
- 我在 Windows 完成上述并截图;移交后你在 macOS `npx github:you/attend`(或 `npm start`)冒烟。

## 风险 / 备注

- Python → TS 是逻辑移植,需逐函数对照 daemon.py;移植后用单测锁行为。
- `npm publish` 与否不影响实现,留最后决定;默认先支持 GitHub-npx + 本地。
- 全程 Windows 验证;macOS 真机冒烟需你执行。
- Codex schema 未知时只给 stub,不伪造数据。
- 旧 `daemon.py` 移入 `legacy/` 保留,不删除。
```
</details></tool-use>

As of 2026-05-30, `attend` (D:\workspace\projects\attend) is being rewritten from a single-file Python/Flask `daemon.py` into a **Node + TypeScript** local web app, distributable via `npx` (npm package with a `bin` entry + `dist/` build; supports `npx github:you/attend`, optional `npm publish`, and local `npm start`).

Target architecture: `src/core/` (pure logic, vitest-tested) decoupled from `src/server.ts` (Hono, server-rendered HTML reusing the old Jinja templates' HTML/CSS); `SessionSource` interface as the vendor extension point (claude impl + codex stub). Old `daemon.py` moves to `legacy/`, not deleted.

Design invariants from DESIGN.md still hold: pull-not-push, descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007), vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution, single polling surface (localhost). Tauri was explicitly rejected (it's a downloadable native app — opposite of zero-install). Memory model unchanged — see [[user-prefers-zero-install-npx]]. For `attend`, the user explicitly chose a **zero-install, `npx`-runnable local web app** (start a local process, open in browser) over a downloadable native app — they rejected Tauri once they realized it means installing an app.

**Why:** the value is "no download, just run from a remote repo." Convenience of `npx <remote>` over installers matters to them.

**How to apply:** default to npx/Node distribution for their tooling. When proposing a stack or distribution mechanism, proactively explain the concepts and concrete costs (e.g. npm account, publish vs GitHub-npx vs local) rather than just naming the tool — they asked "解释 npm npx node" and "这是什么成本". They engage with tradeoff explanations and pick deliberately. Keep their minimalist taste (see DESIGN.md): no heavy frameworks. Related: [[attend-node-pivot]]

/** Shared domain types. session = cache, brief = state. */

export type Pattern = "fresh" | "avoidance" | "stalled" | "healthy" | "active" | "unknown";

export interface BriefFrontMatter {
  status?: string;
  defer_until?: string | null;
  last_touch?: string | null;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface Brief {
  path: string;
  projectDir: string;
  name: string;
  frontMatter: BriefFrontMatter;
  what: string;
  accept: string;
  next: string;
  /** active | deferred | done */
  status: string;
  deferUntil: string | null;
}

/** One vendor session, normalized across Claude / Codex. */
export interface RawSession {
  path: string;
  vendor: string;
  cwd: string | null;
  /** epoch ms, or null when no timestamps were found */
  firstTs: number | null;
  lastTs: number | null;
  prompts: number;
  actions: number;
}

export interface Telemetry {
  sessions: number;
  prompts: number;
  actions: number;
  totalMinutes: number;
  avgSessionMin: number | null;
  lastActionAgeDays: number | null;
  /** ISO string of most recent touch, or null */
  lastTouch: string | null;
  lastTouchAgeDays: number | null;
}

export interface RankedBrief {
  brief: Brief;
  telemetry: Telemetry;
  score: number;
  reason: string;
  pattern: Pattern;
}

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";
import type { Brief, BriefFrontMatter } from "./types.js";

/**
 * Minimal flat YAML front-matter parser. The brief format only uses flat
 * `key: value` pairs (status / defer_until / last_touch), so a full YAML
 * dependency is unnecessary. Mirrors daemon.py's `yaml.safe_load` for that shape.
 */
function parseFrontMatter(raw: string): BriefFrontMatter {
  const fm: BriefFrontMatter = {};
  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const m = /^([A-Za-z_][\w-]*)\s*:\s*(.*)$/.exec(line);
    if (!m) continue;
    const key = m[1] as string;
    let value = (m[2] ?? "").trim();
    if (
      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))
    ) {
      value = value.slice(1, -1);
    }
    fm[key] = value === "" ? null : value;
  }
  return fm;
}

/** Split markdown body into sections keyed by the first token of each heading. */
function parseSections(body: string): Record<string, string> {
  const sections: Record<string, string> = {};
  let current: string | null = null;
  let buf: string[] = [];
  const flush = () => {
    if (current !== null) sections[current] = buf.join("\n").trim();
  };
  for (const line of body.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const m = /^#+\s+(\S+)/.exec(line);
    if (m) {
      flush();
      current = (m[1] as string).toLowerCase();
      buf = [];
    } else {
      buf.push(line);
    }
  }
  flush();
  return sections;
}

/** Parse a brief.md from its text. Only what/accept/next sections are surfaced. */
export function parseBriefText(filePath: string, text: string): Brief {
  let fm: BriefFrontMatter = {};
  let body = text;
  if (text.startsWith("---")) {
    const end = text.indexOf("\n---", 3);
    if (end > 0) {
      fm = parseFrontMatter(text.slice(3, end));
      body = text.slice(end + 4).replace(/^\s+/, "");
    }
  }
  const sections = parseSections(body);
  const projectDir = path.dirname(filePath);
  const statusRaw = fm.status;
  return {
    path: filePath,
    projectDir,
    name: path.basename(projectDir),
    frontMatter: fm,
    what: sections.what ?? "",
    accept: sections.accept ?? "",
    next: sections.next ?? "",
    status: typeof statusRaw === "string" && statusRaw ? statusRaw : "active",
    deferUntil: (fm.defer_until as string | null | undefined) ?? null,
  };
}

export function parseBrief(filePath: string): Brief | null {
  try {
    return parseBriefText(filePath, fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8"));
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

const IGNORED_DIRS = new Set([
  "node_modules",
  ".git",
  "dist",
  ".specstory",
  ".venv",
  "__pycache__",
  ".next",
  "target",
]);

/** Recursively find files named `filename` under `root`, skipping noisy dirs. */
export function walkFor(root: string, filename: string, maxDepth: number): string[] {
  const found: string[] = [];
  const walk = (dir: string, depth: number) => {
    if (depth > maxDepth) return;
    let entries: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      entries = fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      return;
    }
    for (const entry of entries) {
      const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
        if (!IGNORED_DIRS.has(entry.name)) walk(full, depth + 1);
      } else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name === filename) {
        found.push(full);
      }
    }
  };
  walk(root, 0);
  return found;
}

/** Scan every vault root for brief.md files (de-duped, case-insensitive path). */
export function scanVault(vaultRoots: string[], maxDepth = 8): Brief[] {
  const briefs: Brief[] = [];
  const seen = new Set<string>();
  for (const root of vaultRoots) {
    if (!fs.existsSync(root)) continue;
    for (const file of walkFor(root, "brief.md", maxDepth)) {
      const key = file.toLowerCase();
      if (seen.has(key)) continue;
      seen.add(key);
      const b = parseBrief(file);
      if (b) briefs.push(b);
    }
  }
  return briefs;
}

```

```typescript
import type { Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

/**
 * Map telemetry to a behavioral pattern. Thresholds ported 1:1 from daemon.py
 * `classify_pattern`. Labels are descriptive observations, never verdicts
 * (DESIGN.md invariant 3, Steel 2007).
 */
export function classifyPattern(tel: Telemetry): Pattern {
  const { sessions, actions, prompts, avgSessionMin, lastTouchAgeDays } = tel;
  if (sessions === 0) return "fresh";
  if (prompts >= 5 && actions === 0) return "avoidance";
  if (actions === 0 && lastTouchAgeDays !== null && lastTouchAgeDays >= 7) return "stalled";
  if (
    actions > 0 &&
    avgSessionMin !== null &&
    avgSessionMin >= 10 &&
    lastTouchAgeDays !== null &&
    lastTouchAgeDays <= 3
  ) {
    return "healthy";
  }
  if (actions > 0) return "active";
  return "unknown";
}

```typescript
import path from "node:path";
import type { Brief, RawSession, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

const DAY_MS = 86_400_000;
const MIN_MS = 60_000;

/** True if `child` is the same as or nested under `parent`. */
function isInside(parent: string, child: string): boolean {
  const rel = path.relative(parent, child);
  return rel === "" || (!rel.startsWith("..") && !path.isAbsolute(rel));
}

/** Sessions match a brief when its project dir contains, or is contained by, the session cwd. */
export function matchSessions(brief: Brief, sessions: RawSession[]): RawSession[] {
  const proj = path.resolve(brief.projectDir);
  const matched: RawSession[] = [];
  for (const s of sessions) {
    if (!s.cwd) continue;
    const cwd = path.resolve(s.cwd);
    if (isInside(cwd, proj) || isInside(proj, cwd)) matched.push(s);
  }
  return matched;
}

const EMPTY: Telemetry = {
  sessions: 0,
  prompts: 0,
  actions: 0,
  totalMinutes: 0,
  avgSessionMin: null,
  lastActionAgeDays: null,
  lastTouch: null,
  lastTouchAgeDays: null,
};

export function telemetryForBrief(
  brief: Brief,
  sessions: RawSession[],
  now: number = Date.now(),
): Telemetry {
  const matched = matchSessions(brief, sessions);
  if (matched.length === 0) return { ...EMPTY };

  let totalMinutes = 0;
  const durations: number[] = [];
  let lastActionTs: number | null = null;
  let lastTouchTs: number | null = null;
  let prompts = 0;
  let actions = 0;

  for (const s of matched) {
    prompts += s.prompts;
    actions += s.actions;
    if (s.firstTs !== null && s.lastTs !== null) {
      const d = (s.lastTs - s.firstTs) / MIN_MS;
      totalMinutes += d;
      durations.push(d);
    }
    if (s.lastTs !== null && (lastTouchTs === null || s.lastTs > lastTouchTs)) {
      lastTouchTs = s.lastTs;
    }
    if (s.actions > 0 && s.lastTs !== null && (lastActionTs === null || s.lastTs > lastActionTs)) {
      lastActionTs = s.lastTs;
    }
  }

  return {
    sessions: matched.length,
    prompts,
    actions,
    totalMinutes,
    avgSessionMin: durations.length
      ? durations.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / durations.length
      : null,
    lastActionAgeDays: lastActionTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - lastActionTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
    lastTouch: lastTouchTs !== null ? new Date(lastTouchTs).toISOString() : null,
    lastTouchAgeDays: lastTouchTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - lastTouchTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
  };
}

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";

const STOPWORDS = new Set([
  "the",
  "and",
  "with",
  "this",
  "that",
  "from",
  "have",
  "will",
  "name",
  "description",
  "metadata",
  "type",
  "memory",
  "file",
]);

const CJK = /[一-鿿]{2,}/g;
const LATIN = /\b[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{3,}\b/g;

/**
 * Discover Claude Code per-project memory files. Memory model is unchanged from
 * Claude: each project keeps its own `memory/MEMORY.md` under
 * ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/. We union them as the user's memory corpus.
 */
export function discoverMemorySources(claudeProjects: string): string[] {
  const sources: string[] = [];
  let entries: fs.Dirent[];
  try {
    entries = fs.readdirSync(claudeProjects, { withFileTypes: true });
  } catch {
    return sources;
  }
  for (const entry of entries) {
    if (!entry.isDirectory()) continue;
    const mem = path.join(claudeProjects, entry.name, "memory", "MEMORY.md");
    if (fs.existsSync(mem)) sources.push(mem);
  }
  return sources;
}

/** Extract a deduped keyword set (CJK bigrams+ and latin words) from memory files. */
export function loadMemoryKeywords(sources: string[]): string[] {
  const keywords = new Set<string>();
  for (const src of sources) {
    let text: string;
    try {
      text = fs.readFileSync(src, "utf-8");
    } catch {
      continue;
    }
    for (const w of text.match(CJK) ?? []) keywords.add(w);
    for (const w of text.match(LATIN) ?? []) {
      if (!STOPWORDS.has(w.toLowerCase())) keywords.add(w);
    }
  }
  return [...keywords];
}

```

```typescript
import { classifyPattern } from "./pattern.js";
import type { Brief, Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

export interface PriorityResult {
  score: number;
  reason: string;
  pattern: Pattern;
}

/** Blocker present in `next`. Refines daemon.py's bare-"等" check so that a
 *  trailing "等" inside parentheticals (e.g. "(作品B 等)") no longer false-fires;
 *  "等" only counts when followed by concrete content. */
function hasBlocker(next: string): boolean {
  const lower = next.toLowerCase();
  if (lower.includes("block") || lower.includes("stuck") || next.includes("卡")) return true;
  return /等[^\s)）.。,，]/.test(next);
}

/**
 * Heuristic priority: memory alignment ×2 + pattern weight + blocker bonus
 * − defer/done penalty. Returns a composed, human-readable reason so the user
 * can override (DESIGN.md: no opaque scores). Ported from daemon.py.
 */
export function evaluatePriority(
  brief: Brief,
  tel: Telemetry,
  memoryKeywords: string[],
): PriorityResult {
  const blob = `${brief.name} ${brief.what} ${brief.next}`.toLowerCase();
  let score = 0;
  const reasons: string[] = [];

  const kwHits = memoryKeywords.reduce((n, k) => (blob.includes(k.toLowerCase()) ? n + 1 : n), 0);
  if (kwHits > 0) {
    score += Math.min(kwHits, 5) * 2;
    reasons.push(`memory aligned (${kwHits})`);
  }

  const pattern = classifyPattern(tel);
  if (pattern === "avoidance") {
    score += 4;
    reasons.push("avoidance signal — needs decision, not work");
  } else if (pattern === "stalled") {
    score += 3;
    reasons.push("stalled — needs unblock or kill");
  } else if (pattern === "fresh") {
    score += 1;
    reasons.push("fresh — no entries yet");
  } else if (pattern === "active") {
    score += 0.5;
  } else if (pattern === "healthy") {
    score -= 1;
    reasons.push("healthy — in flow, don't interrupt");
  }

  if (brief.status === "deferred") {
    reasons.push(brief.deferUntil ? `deferred until ${brief.deferUntil}` : "deferred without condition");
    score -= 2;
  } else if (brief.status === "done") {
    score = -100;
    reasons.push("done");
  }

  if (hasBlocker(brief.next)) {
    score += 1;
    reasons.push("explicit blocker in next");
  }

  return { score, reason: reasons.join("; ") || "no signal", pattern };
}

```

import type { Brief } from "./types.js";

export type Vendor = "claude" | "codex";

function quote(s: string): string {
  return s.replace(/\\/g, "\\\\").replace(/"/g, '\\"');
}

/**
 * Build a copy-paste spawn command. v0 never auto-spawns — the dashboard only
 * renders the command for the user to paste (DESIGN.md: avoid the 4-tab cascade).
 */
export function spawnCommand(brief: Brief, vendor: Vendor): string {
  const summary = brief.what.trim();
  const next = brief.next.trim();
  const parts: string[] = [];
  if (summary) parts.push(summary);
  if (next) parts.push(`\\n\\nNext: ${next}`);
  const body = quote(parts.join(""));
  return `cd "${brief.projectDir}"\n${vendor} "${body}"`;
}

import type { RawSession } from "../types.js";
import { ClaudeSource } from "./claude.js";
import { CodexSource } from "./codex.js";

/**
 * A vendor transcript backend. New vendors = new implementation; everything
 * downstream (telemetry, priority, UI) is vendor-neutral (DESIGN.md invariant 4).
 */
export interface SessionSource {
  readonly vendor: string;
  scan(): RawSession[];
}

export interface SessionSourceConfig {
  claudeProjects: string;
  codexSessions: string;
}

export function buildSources(config: SessionSourceConfig): SessionSource[] {
  return [new ClaudeSource(config.claudeProjects), new CodexSource(config.codexSessions)];
}

/** Collect sessions from every vendor source. */
export function collectSessions(config: SessionSourceConfig): RawSession[] {
  return buildSources(config).flatMap((s) => s.scan());
}

export { ClaudeSource } from "./claude.js";
export { CodexSource } from "./codex.js";

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";
import type { RawSession } from "../types.js";
import type { SessionSource } from "./index.js";

/** Tool uses that count as productive "actions". */
const ACTION_TOOLS = new Set(["Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit", "Bash", "PowerShell"]);

function parseTs(value: unknown): number | null {
  if (typeof value !== "string") return null;
  const t = Date.parse(value);
  return Number.isNaN(t) ? null : t;
}

interface JsonlEntry {
  cwd?: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  type?: string;
  message?: { content?: unknown };
}

function isTextPrompt(content: unknown): boolean {
  if (typeof content === "string") {
    return content.trim() !== "" && !content.startsWith("<");
  }
  if (Array.isArray(content)) {
    return content.some((c) => c && typeof c === "object" && (c as { type?: string }).type === "text");
  }
  return false;
}

function countActions(content: unknown): number {
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) return 0;
  let n = 0;
  for (const c of content) {
    if (c && typeof c === "object") {
      const block = c as { type?: string; name?: string };
      if (block.type === "tool_use" && block.name && ACTION_TOOLS.has(block.name)) n += 1;
    }
  }
  return n;
}

function parseSessionFile(file: string): RawSession {
  const session: RawSession = {
    path: file,
    vendor: "claude",
    cwd: null,
    firstTs: null,
    lastTs: null,
    prompts: 0,
    actions: 0,
  };
  let raw: string;
  try {
    raw = fs.readFileSync(file, "utf-8");
  } catch {
    return session;
  }
  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    if (!line.trim()) continue;
    let obj: JsonlEntry;
    try {
      obj = JSON.parse(line) as JsonlEntry;
    } catch {
      continue;
    }
    if (session.cwd === null && obj.cwd) session.cwd = obj.cwd;
    const ts = parseTs(obj.timestamp);
    if (ts !== null) {
      if (session.firstTs === null) session.firstTs = ts;
      session.lastTs = ts;
    }
    if (obj.type === "user") {
      if (isTextPrompt(obj.message?.content)) session.prompts += 1;
    } else if (obj.type === "assistant") {
      session.actions += countActions(obj.message?.content);
    }
  }
  return session;
}

/** Reads Claude Code transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl. */
export class ClaudeSource implements SessionSource {
  readonly vendor = "claude";

  constructor(private readonly projectsDir: string) {}

  scan(): RawSession[] {
    const sessions: RawSession[] = [];
    let projects: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      projects = fs.readdirSync(this.projectsDir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      return sessions;
    }
    for (const proj of projects) {
      if (!proj.isDirectory()) continue;
      const dir = path.join(this.projectsDir, proj.name);
      let files: string[];
      try {
        files = fs.readdirSync(dir);
      } catch {
        continue;
      }
      for (const f of files) {
        if (f.endsWith(".jsonl")) sessions.push(parseSessionFile(path.join(dir, f)));
      }
    }
    return sessions;
  }
}

```
